programme.title: Surviving Mann

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: This week, host Laura visits a family who has taken backyard farming to a whole new level. The Killpatrick family led by husband and father Michael is paving the way for the next generation of regenerative farming. This farm sprouted from the 2020 pandemic and in a few short years has become a pivotal cornerstone of their city community for all things holistic and organic. The Farm on Central is proving what can be accomplished with dedication to the future of farming.

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: On this episode, Laura takes a trip to White Oak Pastures in Georgia to meet with pioneer and visionary, Will Harris. After two decades of monocultural farming, Will took his multi-generation farm into a completely new direction by turning it into a regenerative farm. Through trials and tribulations, Will and his family are paving the way for regenerative agriculture and educating the public on the importance of food education.

programme.title: Wild Shots

From tactical holsters to high-speed vehicle drills, Tim puts it all to the test in a live-fire evolution under pressure.

programme.title: Around the ACL
programme.desc: ATA 217: Open #7 Recap, Clauson Breaks Through, and Hodet/Landis Deliver Open #7 in Rhode Island brought major performances, breakout moments, and another shake-up in the season standings. Jake and Anthony kick off this week’s Around the ACL with a quick look at USA Forces in Virginia before diving into the action from Rhode Island. In Doubles, Colin Hodet and Gage Landis add another big win to their season and continue proving why they’re one of the toughest teams in the game right now. Then in Singles, Gabriel Clauson breaks through with a huge performance and continues his rise up the rankings. We also recap the rest of the divisions, including titles from Gina Ramirez, Elizabeth Tennyson / Peyton Haynes, and a massive weekend from Gabriel Clauson and Ethan Farias in Juniors Doubles. Big wins, momentum shifts, and another weekend that changes the conversation heading into the next stretch of the season.

programme.title: Idaho Kid (1960)
programme.desc: A tough, straight-shooting cowboy known as the Idaho Kid rides into a lawless frontier town and finds himself caught between ruthless outlaws and desperate townsfolk. As tensions rise, he must use his wits and gun skills to bring order and protect the innocent, all while confronting his own past.

programme.title: Judge Priest - 1934
programme.desc: In a small Southern town after the Civil War, a wise and folksy judge, played by Will Rogers, presides over local disputes with humor, compassion, and common sense. As he navigates courtroom cases and community tensions, he stands up for fairness and decency while bringing people together in a divided society.

programme.title: Saddle Mountain Roundup (1941)
programme.desc: A determined rancher and his allies stand up against a group of outlaws attempting to seize control of valuable land near Saddle Mountain. As tensions rise, courage and quick thinking are put to the test in a battle to protect rightful ownership and restore peace to the range

programme.title: Saddle Mountain Roundup (1941)
programme.desc: A cowboy steps in to help a ranching community threatened by a gang trying to take control of local land and cattle. As tensions rise in the Saddle Mountain region, he rallies allies and confronts the outlaws in a fight to restore justice and protect the settlers.

programme.title: Six-Gun Rhythm (1939)
programme.desc: A singing cowboy comes to the aid of a young woman who is being cheated out of her rightful inheritance by a gang of crooked outlaws. Using his musical talents and sharpshooting skills, he works to expose the villains and restore justice.

programme.title: Guns of Diablo
programme.desc: Wagon train scout Linc Murdock reminisces about his old feud with rancher Rance Macklin over pretty Maria during the time when Linc worked for the Macklin ranch.

programme.title: Home In Oklahoma (1946, colorized)
programme.desc: Roy edits a small town newspaper. A rancher is murdered, and his fortune is inherited by a young boy.

programme.title: The Law of 45's 1935
programme.desc: In The Law of 45’s, Western star Guinn ‘Big Boy’ Williams blazes onto the screen as Tucson Smith, one of the famed Three Mesquiteers. When land-grabbing crooks terrorize ranchers, Tucson and his partner Stony Brooke (Al St. John) saddle up to take a stand. With blazing six-guns, quick-draw justice, and plenty of frontier grit, this action-packed adventure marks the beginning of the legendary Mesquiteers series.

programme.title: The Gabby Hayes Show - Plumber
programme.desc: Dramatizing the lives of historical figures, such as the naval commander, the lawman and professional gambler, and the outlaw Belle, tall tales and Western scenes

programme.title: The Singing Cowgirl 1938
programme.desc: Dorothy Page saddles up as The Singing Cowgirl, bringing her golden voice and fearless spirit to the frontier! When rustlers and schemers threaten her family’s ranch, she fights back with courage, charm, and a song in her heart. Packed with action, romance, and toe-tapping tunes, this Western delivers a fresh twist on cowboy adventure.
